name: Hawaii SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income Hawaii households through the Department of Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL under broad-based categorical eligibility; higher income limits for Hawaii due to cost of living

name: Med-QUEST (Hawaii Medicaid)
description: Hawaii's Medicaid program providing health coverage through managed care plans for low-income residents including QUEST Integration for most enrollees.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 313% FPL; pregnant women up to 191% FPL

name: Hawaii Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to Hawaii workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they actively seek new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation (HHFDC)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable rental housing, down payment assistance, tax credit programs, and housing development for low-income Hawaii residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Legal Aid Society of Hawaii
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Hawaii residents statewide across all islands in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer protection.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Hawaii residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Hawaii Workforce Development - American Job Centers
description: State workforce development centers across all islands providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, and training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Any Hawaii resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Hawaii LIHEAP - Home Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible Hawaii households pay energy bills through the Department of Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level (adjusted for Hawaii cost of living)

name: Aloha United Way 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting Hawaii residents with health and human services resources across all islands.
eligibilitySummary: Any Hawaii resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Hawaii Foodbank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving Oahu and Kauai distributing food through a network of partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance on Oahu and Kauai

name: Maui Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving Maui County distributing food to partner agencies and programs for food-insecure residents on Maui, Molokai, and Lanai.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in Maui County

name: Hawaii Food Basket - Big Island
description: Feeding America affiliate serving Hawaii Island (Big Island) with food distribution through partner pantries, meal programs, and mobile food distributions.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance on the Big Island

name: Hawaii Office of Veterans Services
description: Assists Hawaii veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, employment, housing, and access to the state veterans home.
eligibilitySummary: Hawaii veterans and their families

name: Hawaii Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support, crisis intervention, and referrals for individuals experiencing mental health or emotional crises across all islands.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Hawaii experiencing a mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress

name: Hawaii Child Care Connection
description: Childcare subsidy and resource program helping low-income working families find and afford quality childcare services across all Hawaii islands.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% of state median income

name: Hawaii WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, and health referrals on all islands.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Hawaii TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program providing cash assistance and supportive services to very low-income families with children in Hawaii.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: TheBus - Honolulu Public Transit
description: Public bus system serving Oahu with extensive fixed routes and TheHandi-Van paratransit, providing affordable transportation across the island.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone on Oahu; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, youth, and Medicare cardholders
hours: Daily service; routes vary from approximately 4:00 AM to midnight

name: Hawaii Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income Hawaii homes to reduce energy costs, focusing on cooling and ventilation improvements given the tropical climate.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Hawaii Adult Education
description: Free adult education programs including GED preparation, English language classes, and citizenship preparation offered through community schools statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school
hours: Varies by location; many evening classes available

name: Hawaii Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence across all islands.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in Hawaii

name: Hawaii Public Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents across all Hawaii islands.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families across Hawaii
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income; 50% for vouchers

name: Hawaii Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for Hawaii residents with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Hawaii residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: Hawaii Prepaid Health Care Act
description: Hawaii's unique employer mandate requiring employers to provide health insurance to employees working 20+ hours per week, ensuring broad health coverage.
eligibilitySummary: Employees working 20 or more hours per week for four consecutive weeks

name: Volunteer Legal Services Hawaii
description: Pro bono legal program providing free civil legal assistance through volunteer attorneys for low-income Hawaii residents on Oahu and neighbor islands.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Hawaii residents with civil legal needs who cannot afford an attorney
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Hawaii Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Hawaii residents to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Hawaii Executive Office on Aging
description: Provides services for seniors across all islands including Kupuna Care, caregiver support, senior centers, and long-term care planning.
eligibilitySummary: Hawaii residents age 60 and older and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Kupuna Care has income eligibility; some services available to all seniors

name: Parents and Children Together (PACT) - Honolulu
description: Comprehensive social services organization in Honolulu providing domestic violence services, childcare, family strengthening, and youth programs.
eligibilitySummary: Families, children, and individuals in the Honolulu area needing social services

name: Institute for Human Services (IHS) - Honolulu Homeless Services
description: Honolulu's largest homeless services provider offering emergency shelter, transitional housing, outreach, and supportive services for individuals and families.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in Honolulu

name: Hawaii Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Federal health insurance marketplace where Hawaii residents can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium tax credits.
eligibilitySummary: Hawaii residents without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL

name: Hawaii Community College System - Workforce Programs
description: Workforce development and career training programs through Hawaii's community colleges on all major islands including certificates, apprenticeships, and short-term training.
eligibilitySummary: Adults seeking career training and workforce education
incomeLimitNotes: Financial aid available for qualifying students

name: Maui Bus
description: Public bus system serving Maui island with fixed routes connecting communities across the island at affordable fares.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone on Maui; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and youth

name: Hawaii Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
description: Federally funded rental assistance vouchers administered through the Hawaii Public Housing Authority to help very low-income residents afford housing across all islands.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income
